subhed: During the World Cup, the organization Her Game Too is rolling out a system for reporting sexist and sexual violence in several bars across France. In Saint-Ouen, women describe the ways they are still forced to prove their legitimacy as football fans.

title: How Côte d'Ivoire's late president built secret ties with Africa's beer giant
subhed: Former Ivorian president Henri Konan Bédié held shares – through offshore companies – in Solibra, Côte d’Ivoire's leading alcoholic beverage company and a subsidiary of the French Castel group, as first uncovered by a Le Monde investigation.

title: 250 years of American independence: The retreat from multiracial democracy
subhed: 'America 250' (7/13). A century after its founding, 38 states made up the nation. Deep divisions persisted over how to treat the Black population. Slavery had been abolished, much to the dismay of the Democrats, who were gaining ground. Although the Republicans won the presidential election, their victory came at the cost of institutionalizing segregationist policies in the Southern states.

title: To join the 'system' or overturn it? Far-right leader Bardella's private life and policies raise doubts
subhed: By openly seeking to appeal to economic elites and the right, far-right leader Jordan Bardella breaks with Marine Le Pen and her father's historic 'anti-system' hostility. Some party members question his desire to pursue radical policies.

title: Parents outraged by lack of school guidelines during heatwave: 'We've had to scramble to make childcare arrangements'
subhed: As classroom conditions worsen, parents have tried to adapt by working from home, taking time off or relying on other adults for help. All have criticized the authorities' lack of preparation and have expressed concern for the years ahead.

title: In the age of AI, Chinese universities overhaul their curricula
subhed: Universities across China are undergoing a major transformation to align with the economic priorities of the Chinese government and to tackle youth unemployment. To survive, some disciplines, such as foreign languages, now have to attach themselves to hard sciences.
